page: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache per core, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8a.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached workloads, with small-block read bandwidth reaching top-tier levels, though large-block uncached read and write operations fall into the average range. The instance also excels in lightweight LLM inference, achieving top-tier token generation speeds with small 135M models, while maintaining average throughput for medium 7B models.

From a resource density perspective, the m8a.4xlarge offers a balanced general-purpose profile. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores deliver high compute efficiency without the overhead of hyperthreading, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps. This hardware configuration is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, image processing, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, the instance includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside high throughput for static web serving and small-model LLM inference. The system delivers balanced resource allocation with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This profile makes the m8a.4xlarge highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, text and image processing, and lightweight machine learning tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-frequency dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for predictable, compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and image processing benchmark performance
Features 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for static web serving, text processing, and small-model LLM inference
